Another union affiliated with the
Central Labor union last night, the
Switchmen of North America send
ing delegates. This is the first O:
the unions actually engaged in NOV
ing trains to affiliate with the Spo-
kane central body, an dtheir advent
into the big organization was the
cause of considerable good feeling
on the part of the delegates as
sembled.


Communications were received
from the State Federation of Labor
giving an account of the defeat of
the eight hour law and the initia-
tive and referendum amendment.
The names of the Spokane men,
bers of the senate and house who
voted to defeat the bills, Stancher,
Congleton, Brown. Rosenhaupt and
Gilbert, were given.


The Plumbers and the Press
mems unions sent in communications
stating they were in favor of seat
ing a fraternal delegate from the
Ministerial alliance and asking that
favorable action be taken by the
central body in giving the Minis
terial association recognition.


A motion that Mr.. Hindley be in
vited to attend the meetings of the
central body was opposed by e. A
Cooney on the ground the organi.
zation had asked for credentials
from the Ministerial association
and would act on such credentials
when presented. Mr.. Cooney is g
member of the Engineers union
which is favorable to the seating O.
fraternal delegate. and his OBJ


lection was in conformity with The


laws of the central body. 1t wa!
sustained by the chair.

tage by securing from the cit.

| clause in all franchises granted

ture.
